verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is a cytosolic chaperone that translocates to the plasma membrane via lipid-binding (phosphatidylserine interaction) under stress conditions (heat shock, cancer transformation). Although it lacks transmembrane or classical secretory signals, cell-surface biotinylation demonstrates it reaches the extracellular-facing PM in both heat-shocked and cancer cells—contexts where tumor-specific surface display is well-documented and functionally important for immune activation, endocytosis, and tumor survival. This stress/cancer-induced ecto-presentation qualifies as cell_state_induced contextual surface accessibility.
